_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast commodity basket, which averages prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice indexed to 100 in 2015, currently stands at 132. This indicates an overall increase of about 32% since 2015. The rise is uneven across these items and largely occurred in recent periods rather than steadily over the entire timeframe. These figures reflect global market prices as reported by FRED and the IMF.
notes.movers: Coffee has been the primary price mover, rising 93% since 2015, nearly doubling in cost. Orange juice prices are up 22%, while wheat and sugar have seen modest increases of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price changes are driven by weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought affecting arabica coffee regions and issues like citrus greening impacting orange juice. Because these soft commodities trade in relatively thin markets, such disruptions can cause sharp global price movements rather than broad inflation.
notes.why: The headline rise in breakfast commodity prices is chiefly influenced by a few weather-sensitive crops. These conditions can cause extended periods of elevated prices, reflecting supply challenges rather than systemic inflationary pressures. Market sensitivity to environmental factors underscores the volatility inherent in soft commodity prices.
